user.title: Connect Emby server
user.description: Connect Home Assistant to your local Emby server. The API key is stored in Home Assistant and redacted from diagnostics.

data_description.name: Display name of the integration; recommended: EMBi
data_description.host: Local IP address or hostname without http:// or https://
data_description.port: Default: 8096 for HTTP or 8920 for HTTPS
data_description.ssl: Enable only when the local Emby endpoint uses HTTPS
data_description.api_key: Dedicated Emby API key used for playback and device discovery

reconfigure.title: Edit connection
reconfigure.description: Change the server address, port, HTTPS setting, or API key. For security, the stored API key is never displayed. Leave the field empty to keep it. EMBi reloads after saving.

data_description.name: Display name of the integration
data_description.host: Local IP address or hostname without protocol prefix
data_description.port: Port of the local Emby endpoint
data_description.ssl: Enable only when the endpoint uses HTTPS
data_description.api_key: Enter a replacement key only when it should change. Leave empty to keep the stored key.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the Emby server.

abort.already_configured: This Emby server is already configured.
abort.unique_id_mismatch: The new connection belongs to another Emby server.

init.title: Manage EMBi
init.description: Configure which Emby clients appear in Home Assistant and use the optional, explicitly confirmed maintenance tools.

clients.title: Manage Emby devices
clients.description: Choose which clients are created as media players. Ignore rules always take precedence. Example: in ‘Selected devices only’ mode, selecting Living room and Bedroom creates only those two players.

data_description.client_mode: ‘Active playback only’ exposes clients only while they are playing or paused.
data_description.allowed_device_ids: Used only in ‘Selected devices only’ mode. Devices not selected here are then not created as media players.
data_description.ignored_device_ids: These devices are never created, regardless of mode. If a device is both selected and ignored, ignore wins.

clients_bulk.title: Bulk device selection
clients_bulk.description: Apply a controlled bulk action to the devices currently reported by the Emby server.

menu_options.clients_allow_all: Display all current devices as selected devices

clients_allow_all.title: Select all current devices
clients_allow_all.description: Adds all {count} unique client/app identities currently reported by Emby to ‘Devices to display’ and switches to ‘Selected devices only’ mode.

clients_allow_none.title: Clear displayed-device selection
clients_allow_none.description: Clears the current displayed-device selection. The selected mode itself is not changed.

clients_ignore_all.title: Ignore all current devices
clients_ignore_all.description: Adds all {count} unique clients currently reported by Emby to the ignore list. This makes every current EMBi media player unavailable after reload.

data.confirm_bulk: I understand and confirm this bulk change

clients_ignore_none.title: Clear ignore list
clients_ignore_none.description: Removes all devices from the EMBi ignore list. Device mode and displayed-device selection remain unchanged.

ha_cleanup.title: Clean up Home Assistant entries
ha_cleanup.description: Only {count} inactive, revalidated candidates are offered: legacy YAML entries, registry-only entries, or inactive entities already ignored by EMBi. Active legacy, ignored, and normal players are deliberately protected.

data.confirm_cleanup: I confirm removal from the Home Assistant entity registry

data_description.cleanup_entity_ids: Candidates are checked again immediately before removal. Removing a registry entry does not delete the device from Emby. A client can return if EMBi still allows it.

server_cleanup_settings.title: Configure optional server cleanup
server_cleanup_settings.description: Server cleanup is disabled by default. Enable it only when historical device records should be deletable from the local Emby server. Stored API keys are never displayed. If no separate key is stored, EMBi uses the normal connection key.

data_description.server_cleanup_enabled: When disabled, the destructive cleanup menu is hidden.
data_description.server_cleanup_api_key: Optional. Enter a replacement key only when it should change. Leave empty to keep an existing separate key; disable server cleanup once to remove it.

server_cleanup.title: Delete devices from the Emby server
server_cleanup.description: Select from {count} historical device records. This removes records from Emby’s device overview, not media files. A client can register again when used later.

data.add_deleted_to_ignored: Also ignore successfully deleted clients in Home Assistant

data_description.delete_device_record_ids: Each item is one server-side history record and includes app, version, last activity, and a short record ID for unambiguous selection. No deletion occurs before the separate confirmation step.
data_description.add_deleted_to_ignored: Uses the stable reported client ID, so the removed client is not immediately recreated as an EMBi player.

server_cleanup_confirm.title: Confirm permanent server cleanup
server_cleanup_confirm.description: You are about to delete {count} device records from the local Emby server. Enter exactly ‘{phrase}’ and enable the confirmation switch.

data.confirm_delete: I confirm permanent deletion from the Emby server

about.title: About Emby Integration - EMBi
about.description:
EMBi extends Home Assistant’s Emby integration with UI configuration, controlled media-player selection, tools for cleaning up the Home Assistant registry, and optional explicitly confirmed cleanup of historical devices on the local Emby server.

Version {version} · Project: Seger · Based on Home Assistant Emby and pyemby.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the Emby server.
error.invalid_cleanup_auth: The cleanup API key was rejected.
error.confirmation_text_mismatch: The confirmation text does not match.
error.selection_required: Select at least one device record.
error.invalid_selection: The submitted selection is no longer valid. Reopen the dialog and try again.

abort.server_cleanup_disabled: Server cleanup is disabled. Enable it in the cleanup settings first.
abort.server_cleanup_complete: Server cleanup finished: {success_count} successful, {failed_count} failed. Failed records: {failed_devices}
